review_title: Apify saved us tons of time.
description: We plugged Apify into our backend to pull Google Maps reviews for businesses on our platform. Took maybe a day to get the API integration working, which was faster than I expected. The sync runs on a cron job now and we don't really think about it anymore, it just works. We did have to try a couple different actors before finding one that returned clean data consistently, but once we landed on the right one it's been solid. For a two-person team building a SaaS, not having to build and maintain our own scraping infrastructure saved us weeks of work we couldn't afford to spend.
author: Saif R.
pros: The API is dead simple. You send a request, you get structured JSON back. No need to manage proxies, headless browsers, or parsing logic. We picked up a Google Maps reviews actor from their store and had it running in our backend within hours. Data quality is consistent and the response includes everything in one shot: review text, ratings, reviewer profiles, owner responses, place metadata.
cons: Some of the actors in the store aren't great. I tried a couple that had bad documentation or just returned weird data, so it took me a few tries to find the one that actually worked for our use case. Also wish there was a way to estimate how much a run will cost before you actually run it, because I've had a couple surprise bills when I was still tweaking the settings.

review_title: Apify Gold Standard for Web Scraping
description: "My overall experience with Apify has been excellent. It has become our primary tool for data extraction and workflow automation.
author: Moussa A.
pros: The most impressive aspect of Apify is its seamless scalability and the vast library of ready-made actors.
cons: The learning curve can be a bit steep for non-technical users, especially when customizing complex scrapers.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: My first go
description: Apify is a powerful platform for web scraping and automation, suitable for various use cases. While it offers robust features and scalability, users should consider the learning curve, cost, and potential scraping challenges.
author: sid K.
pros: Comprehensive SDK and pre-built actors Scalable and robust infrastructure Integrated proxy support User-friendly tools and interface Flexible automation and data export options
cons: Learning curve for beginners Potential high cost with extensive use Challenges with sophisticated anti-scraping measures Performance variability based on target websites Limited direct support for advanced use cases

review_title: A Core Part of My Data Pipeline
description: Overall, Apify has become a core part of how I run my wholesale real estate business. It replaced expensive list services and days of manual research with automated, on-demand data pulls that feed directly into my acquisition pipeline. The platform isn't the most beginner-friendly out of the box, but once past the initial ramp-up it runs reliably and delivers real ROI. For anyone who needs recurring structured data without a full dev team, it's a strong choice.
author: Valerian B.
pros: The pre-built Actor library is the standout feature — it covers most of the sites I target without needing to build anything from scratch. Pair that with reliable scheduling, seamless data exports, and genuinely useful documentation, and it's a platform that earns its place in my workflow. The time and cost savings compared to manual research or third-party list services have been significant.
cons: The learning curve is real. Understanding compute units, decoding actor-specific pricing structures, and knowing which Actor to use for a given job all take more time than they should. The dashboard also feels cluttered and a bit redundant when you're managing multiple scrapers simultaneously. A cleaner UI would up my overall score.

review_title: Apify Review: Honest Take After Real Production Use
author: Paul A.
pros: Actor Store saves days of work pre-built scrapers for almost every major platform mean you're extracting data in under an hour instead of building from scratch.
cons: Credits don't roll over. Unused monthly credits are lost, which feels punitive during lighter usage months

review_title: Great Price to Value
description: Good! Fullfills our needs for now. It was quick to set up and the actors are mostly reliable. We are pretty happy with the results.
author: Felix R.
pros: It’s cheap and easy to set up, and it offers good value for the price. Especially for low volume monthly requests.
cons: The actors can be unreliable at times, and the overall UX/UI could be improved for a smoother experience.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Essential scraping layer that makes a small-team data product viable
description: We run akce.tech, a Czech dance music event aggregator pulling from 16+ sources. Apify powers roughly half of those — the hardest half: Facebook, Instagram, Resident Advisor, and automated source discovery via Google Search. Everything runs in a single daily cron batch feeding into Claude for AI extraction, then MariaDB and Elasticsearch. Without Apify, this project would either not exist or be a full-time job maintaining fragile scrapers. Productivity impact is enormous — we're a small team and Apify lets us focus on what's differentiated (AI extraction, search UX) instead of fighting UI changes from Meta every week. Value for money is excellent at our scale; user interface and Console visibility are solid for debugging and monitoring runs. Minor friction around actor discovery and schema consistency, but these are growing-pains issues, not fundamental flaws
author: Jakub P.
pros: - Managed scrapers for hostile targets — Facebook Events, Instagram Posts, and Website Content Crawler (for Cloudflare-protected sites like Resident Advisor) absorb maintenance we'd never manage ourselves. - Pay-per-use pricing — fair and predictable for a small-team daily-batch workload; no idle cost. - Ready-made actor store — new data sources go from idea to running pipeline in hours. - Clean API and SDK — scheduled runs, dataset exports, and webhook integration all "just work." - Solid output quality — structured JSON drops straight into our Claude extraction pipeline without glue code.
cons: - Actor discovery is noisy — multiple overlapping actors per platform with weak trust signals (official vs. community, last-updated, production readiness). - Output schemas vary between similar actors — switching an actor means rewriting normalization code. - Cost predictability for long crawls — a dry-run cost estimator would help budgeting. - Partial-failure transparency — when an actor returns fewer items than expected, it's not always clear whether the source had fewer results or something silently broke.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A critical platform for scaling data operations and custom APIs
description: My overall experience has been outstanding. Apify plays a central role in making my operation happen efficiently and at scale. It reduced manual work dramatically, improved the reliability of our data pipelines, and gave us the flexibility to build custom services and APIs directly on top of the platform. The combination of automation, infrastructure, scheduling, monitoring, and custom deployment capabilities makes it much more than just a scraping tool—it’s become an operational backbone for our business.
author: André M.
pros: The biggest advantage of Apify is how easy it makes it to build, automate, scale, and deploy data workflows in production. It has become a critical part of my operation because it allows me to automate large-scale data collection, lead generation, monitoring, and internal integrations with exceptional reliability. What stands out the most is the flexibility of the platform. Beyond using existing actors, I can host my own APIs and custom services directly on Apify, which makes it much easier to centralize business logic and keep my entire stack running in one scalable environment. The infrastructure is highly reliable, security feels solid, deployments are simple, and the UI makes managing tasks, runs, schedules, and logs very straightforward. For the amount of engineering time it saves, the value for money is excellent.
cons: Honestly, there is very little to dislike. If I had to point out one area for improvement, it would be making some advanced configuration and debugging workflows slightly more intuitive for new users. While the platform is extremely powerful, beginners may need a short learning curve to fully unlock its potential. That said, once the workflows are in place, the stability and scalability more than make up for it.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Very useful
description: Overall, it’s been a strong addition to our stack for driving more efficient, data-driven outreach. Happy to share more details or walk you through our setup if helpful.

review_title: A Solid Foundation for Flexible Lead Generation
description: Apify has been a core part of my lead generation stack. It handles list enrichment from multiple tools efficiently, and the range of automations available gives me the flexibility I need to build and scale outreach systems. A few hiccups along the way, but overall it's a tool I genuinely rely on and recommend to others doing serious B2B lead gen. Apify has been a core part of my lead generation stack. It handles list enrichment from multiple tools efficiently, and the range of automations available gives me the flexibility I need to build and scale outreach systems. A few hiccups along the way, but overall it's a tool I genuinely rely on and recommend to others doing serious B2B lead gen.
author: Divij S.
pros: The flexibility Apify offers is what keeps me coming back. It lets me pull and enrich lists from multiple platforms like LinkedIn and Instagram, which is huge for my lead gen setup. Initial setup was surprisingly easy, and I've been pairing it with Claude to extend its capabilities even further. Honestly, it's become a non-negotiable part of my workflow.
cons: The Apollo automations breaking down without any prior warning was a rough experience. I wasn't prepared for that disruption, and a heads-up or a ready alternative would've made a big difference. That kind of thing can throw off your entire outreach pipeline.

review_title: Robust and Scalable Web Scraping Platform for Business Use
description: We’ve had an excellent experience using Apify across multiple data-driven projects. It has enabled us to collect structured data from various sources and stream it directly into our internal AI pipeline for brand sentiment analysis. The automation has saved us both time and engineering resources, while the platform’s flexibility has allowed us to scale without needing major infrastructure changes. It’s become a key part of our data operations.
author: MOHAMAD ALVIN R.
pros: Apify provides a powerful and flexible environment for building custom web scraping workflows. The Actor system is incredibly modular, the platform scales well, and integrations via webhooks or APIs are seamless. Their built-in scheduling, dataset management, and UI make automation easy even at scale.
cons: There is a bit of a learning curve for new users, especially if they don't have experience with JavaScript or backend workflows. While the documentation is solid, more visual tools or low-code components could help non-developers onboard faster.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: My experience with Apify
description: Overall, my experience with Apify has been smooth, fast, and easy to use. The service is stable, and the features are helpful, but the cost can make it a bit difficult to justify for smaller or infrequent projects.
author: hassan A.
pros: I like that Apify offers plenty of useful promos and discounts from time to time, plus their support team is always quick and helpful when I need assistance. The platform feels reliable and well-maintained overall.
cons: The main downside for me is the pricing, which can feel high, especially for smaller projects or occasional use. Costs can add up quickly, and I wish there were more affordable, flexible plans for lighter workloads.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Reliable web data backbone for automation and lead mining
description: Overall my experience with Apify has been very positive. It has replaced a lot of fragile scripts and manual data collection, and now most of our lead and market data flows in automatically on a schedule. Once an actor is stable, it becomes a reusable building block we can drop into new automations and services for clients.
author: Arnold Benedict M.
pros: Apify gives me a reliable web data backbone without running my own scraping infra. Actors handle proxies, captchas, and scheduling, and I just work with clean JSON. It is flexible enough for custom use cases but still easy to plug into tools like Make, CRMs, and databases.
cons: There is a learning curve for non-technical users, especially around actors, datasets, and mapping results. When you manage many actors, the UI can feel a bit crowded, and I would like stronger built-in dashboards and alerting. None of these are deal breakers, but they are areas that could improve.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Strong infrastructure for scalable data workflows
description: Overall, my experience has been very positive. Apify has become a stable part of my workflow for automation and data acquisition. It reduces operational overhead, improves time-to-market for data-driven features, and provides a reliable foundation for production use cases. I would recommend it to teams working with data extraction, automation, or AI-related products.
author: Islam S.
pros: Apify makes it easy to build, deploy, and scale data extraction workflows without managing infrastructure manually. The actor-based architecture is flexible, integrations via API are straightforward, and the platform handles scaling reliably. It saves significant development time compared to building scraping systems from scratch.
cons: Advanced configurations can require some technical knowledge, so there is a learning curve for non-developers. Also, when running large-scale jobs, you need to monitor usage carefully to manage costs efficiently.

review_title: Reliable Automation That Just Works
description: My experience with Apify has been very positive. It removes the heavy lifting from automation by handling scaling, scheduling, and infrastructure behind the scenes. I use it for recurring data workflows and it has been reliable, easy to manage, and quick to deploy. Overall, it’s a solid platform that has saved me a lot of time.
author: Michael C.
pros: Apify makes automation straightforward. The platform handles scheduling, scaling, and storage, so I can focus entirely on building workflows. The UI is clean, the documentation is solid, and everything runs reliably once deployed. For data extraction and recurring tasks, it saves a huge amount of time.
cons: The only thing I’d like to see is more built-in monitoring insights for long-running automations. Not a major issue, just something that would make scaling even smoother.

review_title: Good experience using Apify
description: My overall experience with Apify has been very positive. It's dramatically improved our prospect research and content creation efficiency, reducing manual work. The extensive actor library covers all our needs, though quality varies between different actors. Despite this inconsistency, the platform delivers strong ROI and has become essential for our business operations
author: Valentin Q.
pros: The extensive actor marketplace offers solutions for every scraping need we have, and the best part is they consistently deliver results without technical headaches or frequent failures.
cons: The biggest drawback is that not all actors maintain the same quality standards, leading to unpredictable results and forcing me to test multiple options before finding reliable ones.

review_title: My Apify Review
description: My overall experience with Apify has been very strong. It’s a reliable, well-designed platform that makes web scraping and data automation far easier than building everything from scratch. Once workflows are set up, they run consistently with very little maintenance, and the combination of actors, scheduling, logs, and API integrations fits perfectly into our automation stack.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The thing I like most about Apify is how reliable and scalable it is for data collection. Once an actor is set up, it consistently runs on schedule, handles errors gracefully, and delivers clean structured data without constant maintenance. The platform takes care of all the hard parts—proxy management, retries, dynamic pages, queues—so I don’t have to build that infrastructure myself.
cons: The main downside for me is the pricing predictability. Apify’s usage-based model is flexible, but it can be difficult to estimate costs ahead of time—especially for larger or more complex scraping jobs. Small configuration changes can increase consumption, and it sometimes requires extra monitoring to avoid unexpected charges. A more transparent or easier-to-model pricing structure would make budgeting simpler.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Ease of use is great, not much to complain about.
author: Matt H.
pros: I needed a reliable way to grab LinkedIn data for sales prospecting and monitoring job listings. Apify's pre-built actors made setup surprisingly painless, just plug in your parameters and go. The pricing model works well for my use case since I only pay for what I actually use. Most importantly, it just works. I've had consistent results without babysitting the runs.
cons: No real complaints. Setup was easy, runs have been consistent, and I haven't had to troubleshoot anything unexpected

review_title: Reliable Automation for Collecting and Structuring Review Data
description: Overall, Apify has been a very positive experience. It has saved us a huge amount of time, reduced errors, and given us consistent access to customer review data across multiple platforms. Having structured, up-to-date data helps us understand customer sentiment better, react quickly to feedback, and benchmark against competitors. While there are a few areas where usability could be smoother, the benefits far outweigh the drawbacks.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Apify makes it really easy to automate data extraction. I especially like the actor marketplace, which has many ready-made solutions that save a lot of setup time. Scheduling crawlers and exporting data into clean formats like JSON or CSV is smooth, and it integrates well with other tools. For us, scraping reviews from Tripadvisor, Google Maps, and LinkedIn became a reliable, automated process instead of a manual task.
cons: When websites update their layout, crawlers can break and need adjustments. It’s manageable, but it does take some effort. Also, if you want to build or customize advanced actors, there’s a bit of a learning curve if you’re not technical. Pricing can be a little tricky to estimate as usage scales, though it’s still good value overall.

review_title: Really great tool
author: Nicolas B.
pros: I've used this tool for multiple purposes: scraping tiktok profiles, emails from websites, enriching people information, scraping comments from many sites like twitter or instagram, getting information from linkedin profiles, etc. It works great, has multiple payment options and its really flexible. Plus, the API works great to connect it with services like n8n to automate things
cons: Better payment options and sometimes it's not that easy to find an "actor" that fits your purposes exactly how you want them

review_title: I love Apify
description: very satisfied with this tool! I can literally recommend this tool to everybody who is working with contacts.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love Apify! It's the best scraping tool I know. No more tedious manual contact research on the internet. I am grateful that Apify exists. The price-performance ratio is right. I use Apify in n8n workflows - works great.
cons: However, you have to check carefully which scraper you use and how expensive it is. It may happen that you choose an expansive scraper inside Apify even if there are others doing the same.

review_title: Is Apify good choice for developers who are product owners?

Seamless API integration, scalable infrastructure, and a generous free tier make it stand out in a crowded market. Highly recommended for anyone serious about automation and scraping at scale!

The variety of ready-made actors (bots) saved me hours of coding, and the ability to build custom solutions means I’m never limited.
